groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Brand, offer, type, goal, audience, problem, promise, CTA, URL, language, page depth.

groups.title: Audience & Positioning
groups.description: Context, outcomes, differentiators, awareness, objections, alternatives.

groups.title: Offer & Conversion
groups.description: Benefits, features, pricing, conversion path, secondary CTA, urgency.

groups.title: Brand, Proof & Compliance
groups.description: Voice, proof, regulated topics, jurisdiction, disclosures, privacy notes.

groups.title: SEO & Output Settings
groups.description: Keywords, FAQ mode, and additional requirements.

variables.label: Brand or company
variables.placeholder: Example: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Enter the brand, company, project, or creator name behind the offer.

help.why: It identifies who is making the offer and supports accurate brand references.
help.example: Creatornivo
help.avoid: Inventing a brand name.

variables.label: Offer name
variables.placeholder: Example: AI Content Template Library
variables.hint: Name the product, service, application, event, resource, or campaign.

help.why: The landing page must clearly identify what is being presented.
help.example: AI Content Template Library
help.avoid: Vague working titles without a real offer name.

variables.label: Offer type
variables.placeholder: Select the closest offer type
variables.hint: This changes the page structure, terminology, proof needs, and conversion flow.

help.why: Different offer types require different explanations, sections, and calls to action.
help.example: Select the closest offer type
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Primary page goal
variables.placeholder: Example: Get visitors to start a free trial
variables.hint: Describe the single most important action or result for this page.

help.why: The goal determines the message hierarchy and dominant CTA.
help.example: Example: Get visitors to start a free trial
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Example: Solo creators who need faster content production without hiring a team
variables.hint: Describe the people or organizations this offer is specifically for.

help.why: Audience details materially affect language, benefits, objections, and positioning.
help.example: Example: Solo creators who need faster content production without hiring a team
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Core problem
variables.placeholder: What frustrating, costly, slow, or difficult situation does the offer address?
variables.hint: Focus on the main problem the visitor already recognizes or experiences.

help.why: The page needs an accurate problem context without inventing audience pain points.
help.example: What frustrating, costly, slow, or difficult situation does the offer address?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Main value proposition
variables.placeholder: Explain the main value or outcome without making an unsupported guarantee
variables.hint: State why the offer matters and what useful change it can help create.

help.why: It provides the central promise around which the page is structured.
help.example: Clearer first drafts without inventing claims
help.avoid: Unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Primary CTA
variables.placeholder: Example: Start Free Trial
variables.hint: Leave as Auto to generate the clearest action for the selected goal.

help.why: It lets the user control the exact primary action when required.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: CTA destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/signup
variables.hint: Add the page, form, checkout, booking, download, or signup destination.

help.why: It helps keep CTA references consistent with the real conversion destination.
help.example: https://example.com/signup
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Polish
variables.hint: Enter the language in which the landing page should be written.

help.why: A free-text language field supports multilingual output without a restrictive language list.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Page depth
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred page depth
variables.hint: Auto selects the shortest depth that explains the offer and handles key objections.

help.why: It controls content depth without asking the user for a technical word count.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Audience context
variables.placeholder: Describe their situation, priorities, experience, or buying context
variables.hint: Add details that affect how the audience evaluates this offer.

help.why: It enables more precise messaging without splitting audience information across several minor fields.
help.example: Describe their situation, priorities, experience, or buying context
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Desired audience outcome
variables.placeholder: What does the audience want to achieve, improve, avoid, or simplify?
variables.hint: Describe the practical outcome from the audience’s perspective.

help.why: It distinguishes the audience’s desired result from the company’s conversion goal.
help.example: What does the audience want to achieve, improve, avoid, or simplify?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Key differentiators
variables.placeholder: What makes this offer meaningfully different from common alternatives?
variables.hint: Use specific, supportable differences rather than broad superiority claims.

help.why: Specific differentiators materially improve positioning and conversion clarity.
help.example: What makes this offer meaningfully different from common alternatives?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Audience awareness
variables.placeholder: How familiar is the audience?
variables.hint: Auto infers the appropriate explanation level from the other inputs.

help.why: Awareness level changes whether the page should educate, differentiate, or focus on action.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Main objections
variables.placeholder: Example: Price, setup time, trust, compatibility, effort, or switching concerns
variables.hint: List the genuine reasons a suitable visitor may hesitate.

help.why: Objection handling is central to landing-page performance and cannot always be inferred safely.
help.example: Example: Price, setup time, trust, compatibility, effort, or switching concerns
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Alternatives or competitors
variables.placeholder: Describe current solutions, manual methods, or named competitors when relevant
variables.hint: Used only for accurate positioning, not unsupported attacks or comparisons.

help.why: It clarifies the visitor’s current options and the offer’s place among them.
help.example: Describe current solutions, manual methods, or named competitors when relevant
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Key benefits
variables.placeholder: List the most important practical benefits for the customer
variables.hint: Focus on useful outcomes rather than broad promotional claims.

help.why: Supplied benefits prevent the model from inferring unsupported customer outcomes.
help.example: List the most important practical benefits for the customer
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Features or deliverables
variables.placeholder: List included features, services, files, sessions, modules, or deliverables
variables.hint: Include only items that are currently available or confirmed.

help.why: The page must accurately explain what the visitor receives.
help.example: List included features, services, files, sessions, modules, or deliverables
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Pricing and terms
variables.placeholder: Add verified pricing, billing, trial, refund, eligibility, or contract details
variables.hint: Leave blank when pricing should not appear or has not been finalized.

help.why: Commercial details must come from the user and must not be fabricated.
help.example: Add verified pricing, billing, trial, refund, eligibility, or contract details
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Conversion path
variables.placeholder: What happens after the CTA?
variables.hint: This controls button wording, friction level, process explanation, and privacy context.

help.why: The action after the click determines how the page should set expectations.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Secondary CTA
variables.placeholder: Example: View Demo
variables.hint: Use only when visitors need a lower-commitment alternative.

help.why: Some landing pages benefit from one subordinate action, but it should not be generated automatically in every case.
help.example: Example: View Demo
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Time-sensitive offer
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Enable only when a real deadline, quantity, eligibility rule, or capacity limit exists.

help.why: It prevents routine or fabricated urgency from being added to the page.
help.example: Off
help.avoid: Fake urgency without real deadlines.

variables.label: Deadline or availability
variables.placeholder: State the exact deadline, quantity, capacity, eligibility, and relevant conditions
variables.hint: Only verified and concrete limitations may appear in the generated copy.

help.why: Real urgency requires factual details that the model cannot infer.
help.example: State the exact deadline, quantity, capacity, eligibility, and relevant conditions
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest voice
variables.hint: Select the general voice; add finer details in Brand style notes.

help.why: A simple voice selection changes the wording without creating an excessive style questionnaire.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Brand style notes
variables.placeholder: Add preferred wording, terminology, examples, or brand phrases to avoid
variables.hint: Use this for brand-specific guidance that cannot be inferred safely.

help.why: It supports real brand consistency beyond a generic tone preset.
help.example: Add preferred wording, terminology, examples, or brand phrases to avoid
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Verified proof available
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Enable when you can supply testimonials, results, statistics, clients, awards, or certifications.

help.why: It prevents the automatic generation of fabricated trust signals.
help.example: Off
help.avoid: Invented testimonials or ratings.

variables.label: Proof details
variables.placeholder: Paste approved testimonials, statistics, results, credentials, or attribution details
variables.hint: Include exact wording, numbers, sources, dates, qualifiers, and permissions where relevant.

help.why: Proof must be supplied accurately and preserved without invention or material distortion.
help.example: Paste approved testimonials, statistics, results, credentials, or attribution details
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Regulated topic
variables.placeholder: Select only when relevant
variables.hint: This activates more cautious claim, advice, and disclosure handling.

help.why: High-stakes subjects require different claim standards and may depend on jurisdiction.
help.example: None or general
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved wording.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: Example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California
variables.hint: Enter the country, region, or market whose rules may affect the copy.

help.why: Legal and compliance language can vary materially between jurisdictions.
help.example: Example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Restrictions and disclosures
variables.placeholder: Add mandatory wording, claim limits, exclusions, eligibility rules, or prohibited statements
variables.hint: Combine all required restrictions and disclosures in one place.

help.why: It preserves material conditions while avoiding several overlapping compliance fields.
help.example: Add mandatory wording, claim limits, exclusions, eligibility rules, or prohibited statements
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Privacy and data notes
variables.placeholder: Explain what data is collected, why, consent wording, or relevant privacy conditions
variables.hint: Include only confirmed data practices and approved privacy wording.

help.why: Conversion flows that collect personal data may require accurate expectation and consent language.
help.example: Explain what data is collected, why, consent wording, or relevant privacy conditions
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Primary SEO keyword
variables.placeholder: Example: AI content templates
variables.hint: Add one realistic search phrase when organic search matters.

help.why: It provides search-intent direction without requiring a full SEO questionnaire.
help.example: Example: AI content templates
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Supporting keywords
variables.placeholder: Add a short list of closely related phrases, separated by commas
variables.hint: Use only relevant phrases; the generator will not force every term into the copy.

help.why: Related phrases can improve topic coverage while remaining optional.
help.example: Add a short list of closely related phrases, separated by commas
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: FAQ section
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include FAQs
variables.hint: Auto adds FAQs only when they help resolve meaningful visitor uncertainty.

help.why: A FAQ is useful for some offers but should not be forced onto every landing page.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining implementation requirements or essential context
variables.hint: Use this single field for important information not covered elsewhere.

help.why: It handles legitimate edge cases without creating several miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Add any remaining implementation requirements or essential context
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, unsupported guarantees, or fabricated pricing.
